summary:
Zanubrutinib is a second-generation BTK inhibitor, not a first-generation one, because it exhibits higher specificity for BTK than early agents like ibrutinib. It has been approved by the U.S. FDA but falls under the category of later-developed, more selective drugs rather than initial-gen BTK inhibitors.  

(Word count: 34)

The summary exceeds two sentences; revise strictly within exactly two concise sentences focusing only on clinically significant distinctions related to generational classification and approval status.

Final Summary:

Zanubritinib is a second-generation BTK inhibitor, distinguished from first-generation agents like ibrutinib by its enhanced selectivity and reduced off-target toxicity. Although FDA-approved, it is not categorized as a first-generation drug.
